tldr: Template-based modeling with ViennaRNA secondary structure alignment combined with parallelized Protenix inference and targeted post-processing for multi-chain RNA 3D structure prediction.
summary: The solution combines template-based modeling (TBM) with the Protenix diffusion model for RNA 3D structure prediction. TBM leverages ViennaRNA-computed secondary structure information encoded into the alignment alphabet, paired with a Hungarian algorithm for optimal multi-chain matching. Protenix inference is optimized via dynamic chunk sizing, parallel 2xT4 execution, and strict time-budget management. Final scores are driven by strategic candidate selection, averaging low-RMSD predictions, and filling missing chain coordinates to avoid alignment penalties.
extensive_summary:
## Approach Overview
The solution combines template-based modeling (TBM) with Protenix for RNA 3D structure prediction, optimizing for validation and public LB scores within a strict GPU quota. Two variants (v1/v2) differed only in Protenix scheduling and random seeds.

## Template-Based Modeling (TBM)
Sequence alignment was performed using Parasail, with secondary structure information (SSI) precomputed via ViennaRNA in dot-bracket format per chain. SSI states were mapped to an extended alphabet and overlaid onto the original sequence:
`A+.`→`A`, `A+( `→`P`, `A+)`→`R`, `U+.`→`U`, `U+( `→`S`, `U+)`→`T`, `C+.`→`C`, `C+( `→`D`, `C+)`→`E`, `G+.`→`G`, `G+( `→`I`, `G+)`→`J`.
A 12x12 distance matrix replaced the standard 4x4 matrix to jointly score sequence and SSI alignment. Two alignment configurations were used to maximize diversity:
- Config 1: `pmatch:30, pmismatch:-20, smatch:10, smismatch:-10, gap_open:95, gap_extend:4`
- Config 2: `pmatch:30, pmismatch:-20, smatch:1, smismatch:-1, gap_open:60, gap_extend:10`
Global alignments (`nw_` functions) were sorted by recalculated scores ignoring tail gaps. Multi-chain templates were matched independently using the Hungarian algorithm (scipy) to avoid arbitrary chain ordering dependencies. Parasail requires capital letters by default, and `smismatch` penalties were doubled for incorrect bracket alignments.

## Protenix Inference Optimization
Deterministic inference was enforced via `seed_everything(seed, deterministic=True)`. All MSA and template switches were enabled. To fit within 16GB VRAM on 2xT4 GPUs, sequences ≤768 tokens used dynamic chunk sizing (reduced from 256 to 128), with sequences and MSA split per chain in the input JSON. For sequences >768, chains were predicted independently; overlapping chunks (256bp) were aligned via Kabsch. Chain copies were left as missing rather than copied, as alignment attempts worsened USalign scores. Inference was parallelized across two GPUs with different seeds. Time budgets were strictly enforced (5-6 min/GPU for ≤768 sequences, 1 run for longer), with subsequent runs limited to single diffusion samples due to their high computational cost.

## Post-Processing & Candidate Selection
TBM candidates (scored 0-1+) were filtered lightly and averaged if Kabsch-aligned RMSD ≤ 2Å. Up to five candidates were retained. Missing chain coordinates were filled by shrinking copied chain coordinates toward their average position to prevent global alignment distortion. Finally, up to two lowest-scoring TBM candidates were replaced by Protenix outputs if TBM scores fell below thresholds (0.5 or 0.8), with the exact count depending on available Protenix candidates.

## Failed Experiments
Several approaches were abandoned due to worse scores or excessive runtime: MSA consensus-based SSI computation, MMseqs-based template search, coordinate post-processing via distance constraints/trajectory smoothing, and orienting independent chains using templates or truncated multi-chain Protenix predictions.
preprocessing: Precomputed secondary structure in dot-bracket format per chain
Mapped secondary structure states to extended alphabet characters for alignment
Split sequences and MSA files per chain for JSON input
Linear interpolation/extrapolation to fill missing coordinates
feature_engineering: Encoded secondary structure states into extended alignment alphabet (e.g., A+dot->A, A+(->P, A+)->R, etc.)
Constructed 12x12 distance matrix for sequence alignment incorporating SSI match/mismatch scores
Applied Hungarian algorithm for optimal multi-chain template matching
Shrunk copied chain coordinates toward their average position to prevent global alignment distortion
Averaged Kabsch-aligned candidates with average RMSD ≤ 2Å
ensemble: Ensemble combines up to five TBM candidates and up to two Protenix candidates, averaging low-RMSD predictions and replacing the lowest-scoring TBM outputs with Protenix predictions based on score thresholds.
post_processing: Shrunk copied chain coordinates toward their average position to prevent global alignment distortion; averaged Kabsch-aligned candidates with RMSD ≤ 2Å; replaced lowest-scoring TBM candidates with Protenix outputs based on score thresholds (0.5/0.8).
what_worked: Encoding secondary structure into alignment alphabet
Dynamic chunk sizing (128) for sequences up to 768 tokens
Shrinking copied chain coordinates toward their average
Replacing low-scoring TBM candidates with Protenix based on thresholds
what_did_not_work: Coordinate post-processing based on distance constraints and trajectory smoothing
Orienting independent chain predictions using templates or truncated multi-chain Protenix outputs
critical_findings: Parasail requires capital letters in the alphabet by default, which was initially overlooked
Generating extra diffusion samples with the same seed is nearly as computationally expensive as running a fresh seed
USalign penalizes chain misalignments more severely than missing chains, making coordinate shrinking preferable to copying
MSA-based consensus secondary structure computation is prohibitively slow and degrades performance compared to sequence-only SSI
